name: Qa'nab ibn Amr
title: Basran Companion
shortDescription: A warrior from Basra who secretly reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan and was manfully martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Qa'nab ibn Amr al-Namiri was a sincere Shia and warrior from the city of Basra. At that time, Basra was under the strict military control of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad.

Upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) departure, Qa'nab, with exemplary bravery and through secret desert routes, made his way from Basra to Mecca and joined the Master of Martyrs' (AS) caravan there.

He accompanied the Imam throughout the entire journey to Karbala, feeling honored to be with the Prophet's (PBUH) son. On the day of Ashura, with the start of the all-out attack and the firing of heavy arrows by the enemy (known as the first attack), Qa'nab drew his sword.

He stood manfully on the front line of defense and, after a brave battle, attained the grace of martyrdom in that very first attack, his name recorded among the Imam's loyal companions.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى قَعْنَبِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو النَّمِرِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qa'nab ibn Amr al-Namiri.
